english: body which exists only in our imagination (yid (yid)); in that case it would be identical with sgyu-lús (sgyu lus). Comp. lus-rgyágs (lus rgyags) a fat body Cs., lus-ríd (lus rid) a mean, thin, lean body, lus-sbóm (lus sbom) a thick stout figure, lus-ríṅ (lus ring) a long tall body, lus-t'uṅ (lus thung) a short body Cs. - lús-čan (lus can) having a body, hence as sbst. = séms-čan (sems can) creature, being, lús-čan kún-gyi yíd-du ‛oṅ (lus can kun gyi yid du 'ong) a favourite of every creature Stg. - lus - stód (lus stod) upper part of the body, lus-smád (lus smad) lower part of the body. - lus-bóṅs (lus bongs) the bulk of a body. - lus-byád (lus byad) form of the body. - lus-med (lus med) having no body, incorporeal, ghostlike, ghostly, lus-méd-pai skad (lus med pai skad) a ghostly voice Mil. - lus-smád (lus smad) v. lus-stód (lus stod). - lus-zúṅs (lus zungs) v. sub gzuṅs (gzungs).

C. also lás-pa (las pa), to remain behind or at home, bód-du zlá-ba γnyis (bod du zla ba gnyis) to remain in Tibet for two months Glr.; to be remaining or left Dzl.; to be forgotten, omitted, left behind; gyén-du lús-pa (gyen du lus pa) to remain uppermost, floating to remain standing, sitting, lying, e.g. *k'a lús-sa mi dug* (*kha lus sa mi dug*) W. the snow does not remain, will soon melt away; lús-par byéd-pa (lus par byed pa) Pth., lús-su ‛júg-pa (lus su 'jug pa), *lus čúg-če* (*lus cug ce*) Ld. to leave behind, to leave a remainder; ma-lús-par (ma lus par) entirely, wholly, without remainder, without exception, ‛gró-ba ma-lús (ʼgro ba ma lus) or mi-lús (mi lus) Miṅ., all creatures without exception; má-lus-par (ma lus par) prob. also: surely, undoubtedly, at any rate, in any case, in f. - lús-ma (lus ma), rjés-lus (rjes lus), pyír-lus (phyir lus), lhág-lus (lhag lus) Cs. remainder, balance, residue.

1. a small not cultivated river-island C., = glíṅ-ka (gling ka) and zal (zal). - 2. v. leu (le'u). - 3. W. a word expressive of civility and respect, and added to other words or sentences, like Sir! and Madam! in English, *zu-lé* (*zu le*) good day, Sir! it is also added to the word sa-heb (sa heb) gentleman, and then sa-heb-le (sa heb le) is about equivalent to: honoured Sir, dear Sir. - 4. num.: 16.

1. Med. frq., Lex. = གུར་ saffron, whereas Cs. has: 'poppy, le-brgan-rtsí (le brgan rtsi) the juice of poppies, opium, le-brgán-ghi mé-tog (le brgan ghi me tog) the poppy flower, le-brgán-ghi ‛brás-bu (le brgan ghi 'bras bu) poppy-seed', and Sch. adds: le-brgan-mdóg (le brgan mdog) poppy-coloured, light-red, and he translates also le-brgán (le brgan) Dzl. SV, 1, by 'poppy-coloured', although it is mentioned there amongst various species of Lotus. But in W. poppy and opium are usually called by the Hindi name افيم , pim; neither in W. nor in Sik. did I meet with any body, who knew the significations given by Cs. and Sch., but only: 2. diapered design of woven fabrics; thus also Mil.: le-brgán dmár-poi ydan (le brgan dmar poi gdan) a flowered carpet, le-brgán jol-bér (le brgan jol ber) Pth. a flowered dress with a train.
the soft downy wool of goats (esp. those of Jangtháng) below the long hair, the shawl wool; fine woolen-cloth.
Cs.: consequence; Sch.: rebuke, reprimand, reproof, and le-lán-pa (le lan pa), le-lán bdá-ba (le lan bda' ba) to blame, rebuke, reprove; le-lán-čan (le lan can) Cs. consequential, important (?).

indolence, laziness, tardiness, lé-lo ma byed čig (le lo ma byed cig) don't be lazy! Glr.; γčig lé-lo byás-nas (gcig le lo byas nas) as one (of them) had been lazy Dzl.; lé-los k'yer (le los khyer) he is overcome by laziness Mil.; lé-loi rjés-su ‛gró-ba (le lo'i rjes su 'gro ba) to be given to laziness Ld.-Glr.; lé-lo-čan (le lo can) lazy, indolent, slothful. - *le-šól* (*le shol*) W. = le-lo (le lo).

B., légs-po (legs po) and -mo (-mo) C. (cf. also no. 3) 1. good, serving the purpose, with regard to things; adv. légs-par (legs par) well, duly, properly, légs-par ‛tsól-ba (legs par 'tsol ba) to search, to investigate accurately Glr.; bsú-ba légs-po gyis (bsu ba legs po gyis) do care for a proper reception! Glr.; légs-par gyur čig (legs par gyur cig) (Schr. adds kyéd-la (khyed la)) may you prosper! Sch., légs-par ‛óṅs-so (legs par 'ongs so) you are welcome Sch.; happy, comfortable, bdag légs-na (bdag legs na) when I am well off (opp. to nyés-na (nyes na)) Do.; legs nyes stón-pai mé-loṅ (legs nyes ston pai me long) mirror of fate, of the future Glr.; lo(-tog) légs(-pa) (lo(-tog) legs(-pa)) B., *lo lag-mo* (*lo lag mo*) W., a rich, healthy, happy year; čí ltar byás-na legs (ci ltar byas na legs) which is the best way of doing it? Glr.,
